Sorghum is a type of cereal crop, belonging to the grass family (Poaceae), commonly used for food, animal feed, and biofuel production. The health benefits of sorghum include its high nutritional value, with a rich content of fiber, vitamins, and minerals, making it beneficial for digestion and blood sugar control. Sorghum is rich in bioactive constituents such as phenolic acids, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional medicine, sorghum is often used to prepare herbal teas, extracts, and tonics to promote overall health and well-being, and is also used in the production of herbal infusions and supplements.
